description.en: Question 1. Lucy graphed a system of linear equations on a coordinate plane whose x- and y-axes run from −10 to 10 in increments of 1. One line rises from lower left to upper right, passing through (−4, 0), (−1, 3), and (0, 4). The other line falls from upper left to lower right, passing through (−1, 3), (0, 2), and (2, 0). The lines intersect at (−1, 3). What is the solution to the system of equations? Choices: A: (−4, 2) B: (−1, 3) C: (0, 2) D: (2, 4)

Question 2. Which sequence of transformations takes △A to its image, △B? On a coordinate plane whose x- and y-axes run from −10 to 10 in increments of 1, triangle A has vertices (−9, 4), (−8, 9), and (−3, 4). Its horizontal base extends from (−9, 4) to (−3, 4). Triangle B has vertices (3, 2), (8, 7), and (9, 2). Its horizontal base extends from (3, 2) to (9, 2). Choices: A: reflection over the x-axis and translation 2 units down B: reflection over the y-axis and translation 2 units down C: translation 2 units down and 90° rotation about the origin D: translation 12 units right and 90° rotation about the origin

en: A: reflection over the x-axis and translation 2 units down
B: reflection over the y-axis and translation 2 units down
C: translation 2 units down and 90° rotation about the origin
D: translation 12 units right and 90° rotation about the origin

description.en: Question 3. What is the solution to the equation below? 2(x − 3) = 2x + 5. Choices: A: x = 2 3/4 B: x = −2 3/4 C: There is no solution. D: There are infinitely many solutions.

en: D: There are infinitely many solutions

description.en: Question 5. Which graph below does not represent a function of x? Choices: A: A straight line with arrows at both ends rises from lower left to upper right, passing through (0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4), (4, 5), and (5, 6). B: An upward-opening parabola with vertex (0, 0), symmetric about the y-axis, passing through (−2, 4), (−1, 1), (1, 1), and (2, 4), with arrows on both upper ends. C: Six closed points form three horizontal segments: one from (1, 3) to (2, 3), one from (1, 1) to (2, 1), and one from (3, 2) to (4, 2). D: A V-shaped graph with vertex (0, 0), consisting of two rays with arrows that rise leftward and rightward; it passes through (−5, 5), (−1, 1), (1, 1), and (5, 5).

en: A: A straight line with arrows at both ends rises from lower left to upper right, passing through (0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4), (4, 5), and (5, 6)
B: An upward-opening parabola with vertex (0, 0), symmetric about the y-axis, passing through (−2, 4), (−1, 1), (1, 1), and (2, 4), with arrows on both upper ends
C: Six closed points form three horizontal segments: one from (1, 3) to (2, 3), one from (1, 1) to (2, 1), and one from (3, 2) to (4, 2)
D: A V-shaped graph with vertex (0, 0), consisting of two rays with arrows that rise leftward and rightward; it passes through (−5, 5), (−1, 1), (1, 1), and (5, 5)

description.en: Question 6. Simplify: 4⁸/4⁻⁴. Choices: A: 4⁻³² B: 4⁻² C: 4⁴ D: 4¹²
Question 17. Which expression is not equivalent to 6³/6⁶? Choices: A: 1/6² B: 6⁻³ C: 1/216 D: 1/6³

Question 19. A lab has two bacteria cultures. Culture A contains 8 × 10⁴ bacteria, and culture B contains 4 × 10⁶ bacteria. How do the two cultures compare in size? Choices: A: Culture A contains twice as many bacteria as culture B. B: Culture A contains 1/2 as many bacteria as culture B. C: Culture A contains 1/25 as many bacteria as culture B. D: Culture A contains 1/50 as many bacteria as culture B.

en: A: Culture A contains twice as many bacteria as culture B
B: Culture A contains 1/2 as many bacteria as culture B
C: Culture A contains 1/25 as many bacteria as culture B
D: Culture A contains 1/50 as many bacteria as culture B

description.en: Question 49. A water tank is in the shape of a right circular cylinder with a height of 20 feet and a volume of 320π cubic feet. What is the diameter, in feet, of the water tank? Choices: A: 16 B: 10 C: 8 D: 4
Question 50. Which equation does not represent a linear function of x? Choices: A: y = −(3/4)x B: y = x/2 C: y = −3 + 2x D: y = 3x² − 2

Question 51. If △ABC is rotated 90° clockwise about the origin, what will be the new coordinates of vertex B? A coordinate plane has horizontal x- and vertical y-axes, with unit grid lines and labeled values from −5 through 5 on each axis. Triangle ABC has vertices A at (−2, 3), B at (−4, 1), and C at (−1, 1). Segment BC is horizontal from B to C, and segments BA and AC meet at A. Choices: A: (−1, −4) B: (1, 4) C: (4, 1) D: (4, −1)

Question 61. Mr. Wallace surveyed 75 students at Poole Middle School to find out the students’ favorite place to eat lunch. The results are in a table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH.” Columns are Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 16, 21, 37. Girls: 24, 14, 38. Total: 40, 35, 75. Which table shows the approximate relative frequencies of Mr. Wallace’s data? Choices: A: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 16%, 21%, 37%. Girls: 24%, 14%, 38%. Total: 40%, 35%, 75%. B: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 21%, 28%, 49%. Girls: 32%, 19%, 51%. Total: 53%, 47%, 100%. C: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 40%, 60%, 49%. Girls: 60%, 40%, 51%. Total: 100%, 100%, 100%. D: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 43%, 57%, 100%. Girls: 63%, 37%, 100%. Total: 53%, 47%, 100%.

en: A: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 16%, 21%, 37%. Girls: 24%, 14%, 38%. Total: 40%, 35%, 75%
B: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 21%, 28%, 49%. Girls: 32%, 19%, 51%. Total: 53%, 47%, 100%
C: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 40%, 60%, 49%. Girls: 60%, 40%, 51%. Total: 100%, 100%, 100%
D: A table titled “FAVORITE PLACE TO EAT LUNCH,” with columns Cafeteria, Outside, and Total. Boys: 43%, 57%, 100%. Girls: 63%, 37%, 100%. Total: 53%, 47%, 100%
